title: Introduction to Group Theory: Key Concepts and Relationships
description: A flowchart illustrating the foundational concepts of Group Theory and their logical progression, starting from the definition of a group and exploring key properties, substructures, and examples.
elements: Group Definition (Set G, Operation *, Closure, Associativity, Identity, Inverse)
Finite vs. Infinite Groups (Order of a Group)
Cyclic Group (Generated by a single element)
relationships: A Group must satisfy all four group axioms
An Abelian Group is a special type of Group where the operation is commutative
A Group can be classified by its order as either Finite or Infinite
A Subgroup is a subset of a Group that itself forms a Group under the same operation
A Cyclic Group is a type of Group that can be generated by a single element
Common Examples are concrete instances that satisfy the abstract Group definition
